title: Agent Control Contract — Exit-Code Table
description: The FROZEN exit-code table of the Agent Control Contract. Authority: agent RFC 0011 §5 (the table) framed/versioned by RFC 0016 §5.1/§5.2 (podFailurePolicy intent mapping). Reference source of truth: crates/agent/src/exit.rs (EXIT_CODES const, the SUCCESS..DEADLINE consts, SIGKILL_EXIT/SIGTERM_EXIT, once_exit(), pod_failure_intent()). This artifact is a DATA catalogue (not a JSON-document validator) carrying the code -> {neutral name, reference const alias, meaning, podFailurePolicy intent} mapping plus mode reachability. agentctl compiles `intent` into Job onExitCodes / podFailurePolicy rules. Versioned by `exit_codes_version` (== surfaces.exit_codes); additive => minor; any change to a code's meaning or to its intent => major. A consumer MUST refuse only an unknown MAJOR; an unrecognised code defaults to intent `retriable` (never a silent FailJob).
x-version-note: exit.rs EXIT_CODES = "1.0" and RFC 0016 §5.1 pin "1.0". RFC 0014 §5 jsonc sketch literally showed "RFC-0011-§5"; that form is kept only as a documentation alias below. The contract uses the bare major.minor string "1.0".

intent_vocabulary.description: The CLOSED podFailurePolicy intent vocabulary agentctl compiles into onExitCodes (RFC 0016 §5.2). pod_failure_intent() in exit.rs is total over the table and defaults an unrecognised code to `retriable`.

semantics.complete: Treat as success; no retry, no FailJob (Job counts a completion).
semantics.terminal: Permanent failure of THIS spec; do not retry (config/usage error or a reasoned refusal). Maps to FailJob.
semantics.retriable: Transient/unspecified failure; eligible for backoff retry (Ignore toward backoffLimit).
semantics.policy: Budget/partial outcome; verdict is operator policy (default-retriable but tunable via --budget-exit-code).
semantics.infra: Kernel/infra-set (OOM, ungraceful SIGTERM); infrastructure remediation, not an application retry.

codes.name: EXIT_OK
codes.meaning: Success — one-shot completed / loop clean bound / CLEAN SIGTERM DRAIN (a clean drain exits 0, NOT 143).

codes.name: EXIT_FAILURE
codes.meaning: Generic/unspecified failure. once_exit() also maps Cancelled and Crashed here.

codes.name: EXIT_USAGE
codes.meaning: Config/usage error — validation failed before any side effect. Also --validate-config invalid and a bad config file (RFC 0017).

codes.name: EXIT_PARTIAL
codes.meaning: Partial result emitted (a result-body property, not a status). once_exit() maps Stalled/LoopDetected here. Remappable via --budget-exit-code.

codes.name: EXIT_INTELLIGENCE
codes.meaning: Intelligence endpoint unreachable / auth error after retries.

codes.name: EXIT_SEMANTIC
codes.meaning: Agent ran correctly but concluded the task cannot be done / refused (TerminalStatus refused).

codes.name: EXIT_MCP
codes.meaning: A required MCP server failed connect/handshake or died unrecoverably.

codes.name: EXIT_BUDGET
codes.meaning: Hit max-steps/max-tokens/deadline/tree budget before a result (P-cost). once_exit() maps ExhaustedSteps/ExhaustedTokens/Deadline here. Remappable via --budget-exit-code.

codes.name: EXIT_TIMEOUT
codes.meaning: Hard wall-clock deadline tripped (mnemonic to timeout(1)). REACHABLE via the supervisor hard-kill ladder (KillReason::Deadline/Stuck -> exit::DEADLINE in main.rs kill_report_status()); NOT reachable via once_exit(), which folds TerminalStatus::Deadline into BUDGET(7). See x-mapping-divergence.

codes.name: SIGKILL_EXIT
codes.meaning: 128+SIGKILL — OOM / kubelet hard-kill (raise memory limit). Kernel-set; the binary never exit(137)s itself.

codes.name: SIGTERM_EXIT
codes.meaning: 128+SIGTERM — ungraceful exit (drain forced past budget). Kernel-set; DISTINCT from a clean drain, which is 0.

x-mapping-divergence.description: The exit-code TABLE (code -> meaning -> intent) is the wire contract and is identical between RFC 0011 §5 and the reference. The INTERNAL status->code mapping fn once_exit() is impl-internal and NOT part of the contract; it diverges from RFC 0011 §5.2 prose. Prefer the source for what is emitted, but freeze only the table.

once_exit.Completed: 0 (EXIT_OK), or 3 (EXIT_PARTIAL) when a usable partial exists
once_exit.ExhaustedSteps | ExhaustedTokens | Deadl: 7 (BUDGET) — note Deadline folds to 7, so 124 is unreachable via THIS fn (once_exit); 124 IS reachable via the supervisor kill ladder, see code_124 note

x-mapping-divergence.rfc_0011_§5.2_prose: Deadline->124, Cancelled->0 (clean drain), Stalled/LoopDetected->3|1. Code 124 is defined and OS-mnemonic but currently unreachable through once_exit().

x-mode-reachability.description: Informational (RFC 0011 §7 / RFC 0016 §5.4) — which codes a mode can produce. Descriptive, not enforced.
x-mode-reachability.note: exit_code=2 (EXIT_USAGE) is never reachable from a run-outcome report (validation fails before any run). 137/143 are kernel-set and never returned by the binary.

x-budget-exit-code-remap.description: An operator may remap EXIT_PARTIAL(3) and EXIT_BUDGET(7) via --budget-exit-code (RFC 0011 §5.2). The default-retriable/policy disposition is operator-tunable, not a fixed verdict. Relevant to P-cost.

x-sum-types.exit_disposition: In agent://status the exit_disposition field is null | integer(code) — null until terminal, then the integer code.

x-provisional.p_cost: EXIT_BUDGET(7) is the P-cost budget signal; its policy disposition is operator-remappable (see x-budget-exit-code-remap).
x-provisional.code_124_unreachable: The 'unreachable' claim is scoped STRICTLY to once_exit(), which folds TerminalStatus::Deadline into BUDGET(7). 124 IS reachable: the supervisor hard-kill ladder maps KillReason::Deadline/Stuck to exit::DEADLINE (main.rs kill_report_status()), so returned_by_agent=true.
